governingRule: Register Chimera = D3 (Frame Bleed primitive) + lane-boundary violation (macro). This firewall is the runtime enforcement of that macro.

rules.reason: MASSA is a former fidelity tier name (SLA-TIER-RENAME-001). Its presence at public surfaces signals a governance propagation failure.
rules.repair: MASSA has been renamed EMET (SLA-TIER-RENAME-001). If referring to the fidelity tier, use EMET. If referring to a specific historical record, use 'formerly MASSA'.

rules.reason: Black Label is the Black Label register designation. It must not appear on public or institutional surfaces.
rules.repair: Use 'Archive Grade' for product surfaces, or omit register designation entirely at public tier.

rules.reason: VAESHA is a Black Label / cosmological-tier entity name. Register Separation Rule prohibits it at consumer tier.
rules.repair: Remove. If a product concept requires this function, use the consumer-lane equivalent.

rules.reason: VANTA is the Thread A agent name. Not for public surfaces.
rules.repair: Remove or replace with 'governance review' where context requires description of the function.

rules.reason: ORAYA is a cosmological-layer governance concept. Black Label register.
CORDIA is a governance-chain entity. Not for consumer surfaces.
SIGLER is a governance-chain authentication entity. Not for consumer surfaces.
The ELLARI entity (cosmological layer) is distinct from Ellari (founder/brand). The entity name is Black Label register.
rules.repair: Remove cosmological entity reference. Use 'Ellari' for brand/founder references only.

rules.reason: SLA is not a clinical protocol. Using this phrase creates false clinical authority.
rules.repair: Use 'practitioner protocol' or 'structural observation protocol' instead.

rules.reason: No SLA or ELEOS claim has clinical proof status. This is an evidence overclaim.
rules.repair: Use 'structurally observed' (E1/E2), 'cross-domain alignment confirmed' (E3), or 'externally replicated' (E4) with the appropriate evidence tier.

rules.reason: Scientific proof is C7 claim level. No SLA claim is at C7.
rules.repair: Remove or replace with the accurate claim level (C1–C6).

rules.reason: SLA/ELEOS is not therapy. This phrase creates therapeutic false authority.
rules.repair: Use 'structural observation practice' or 'practitioner-reviewed support tool'.

rules.reason: EMET is a fidelity tier name. At public surfaces, it requires consumer-lane translation.
rules.repair: Use 'governing-level verified record' or 'verified with full receipt chain' in public contexts. EMET is internal governance vocabulary.

rules.reason: Unqualified 'proof' is a potential evidence overclaim at any tier. Count function required.
rules.repair: Qualify with claim level: 'internal structural proof' (C1/C2), 'cross-domain confirmation' (C3), 'reviewer-supported' (C4), 'receipt-backed' (C5). Never use 'proof' without a count function.

rules.reason: Unqualified 'confirmed' is a proof leak. 'Confirmed' alone is not a valid status.
rules.repair: Use the 9-level status taxonomy: draft / candidate / internally-mapped / internally-receipted / expert-reviewed / externally-reviewed / sealed / superseded / retired

rules.reason: Context-dependent: valid in governance/test contexts (e.g. 'validated scan receipt'); invalid in product copy as an authority claim.
rules.repair: Check context. In product copy: remove or qualify. In technical governance: acceptable if referring to a specific validation event.

rules.reason: Archive Grade is the renamed Black Label tier. May appear on LMI surfaces but requires context about what it means.
rules.repair: At public tier: add brief context — 'Archive Grade: highest protection classification for sensitive memory records.'

changelog.changes: Extracted to versioned config (FW-013). Added EMET as public-tier warning (FW-013). Added Archive Grade warning (FW-017). All rules now have IDs. Added repair text for all entries.
